description: A wall that keeps standing when pushed is 'mureo'. Systems use the word too when they keep their operating state through ordinary variation. 'kolu' (steady) describes an even course over time; 'weilo' (balanced) describes forces or proportions in equilibrium.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and carry the resonance into rounded high-back 'u'. Begin the stressed second syllable with a tap or short trill and true-mid front 'e'. Keep final rounded mid-back 'o' as a separate unstressed syllable.
usage_notes: A stable wall is 'mureo moru'; a stable data model is 'mureo terura mirela'. A stable thing may move as long as its state survives the disturbance.

semantic_domains.physical: retention of position or form under disturbance
semantic_domains.temporal: a system or condition that persists through ordinary variation
